sourceMovie.title: Tales from Earthsea
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Tales from Earthsea works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Slayers Return
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tales from Earthsea clicked for you, Slayers Return (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Black Cauldron
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tales from Earthsea clicked for you, The Black Cauldron (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The War of the Rohirrim

Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tales from Earthsea?
faq.answer: Try Slayers Return, The Black Cauldron, The Lord of the Rings: The War of the Rohirrim—each shares DNA with Tales from Earthsea while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tales from Earthsea?
faq.answer: Tales from Earthsea (2006) is best described as Animation, Fantasy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tales from Earthsea worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
